tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: They're asking me to redo their part because I'm supposedly better. It's an easy win for the grade, but feels like wasting energy on them instead of my own work.

desires: Finish my own project section before 19:00 so I can get that 'done' checkmark and feel like I actually accomplished something today.
Get this extra task done quickly without any back-and-forth or emotional friction, then mentally archive it as a solved problem so I don't have to think about it tomorrow.
Not look incompetent or pushy when I drop the teammate's work off, but also not waste enough energy redoing their part that I fall behind on my own actual progress.

goals.description: Complete my own project section and hit the 'done' checkmark by 19:00.
goals.rationale: This is the core driver for my sense of accomplishment. The deadline is fixed, so I need to secure that validation before anything else.

goals.description: Quickly process teammate_A's dumped task, give a brief confirmation message to close the loop, then mentally file it away as 'solved'.
goals.rationale: I need to handle this efficiently to avoid friction, but only enough to clear my mind. Pushing back on their offer would be rude and create drama; accepting it without doing deep work saves energy for my actual goal.

agent.last_reasoning: Securing the 'done' checkmark for my own project is my primary driver. I'll finish this fast to hit the 19:00 deadline. I'll briefly text teammate_A confirming receipt of their dump, just enough to avoid friction without doing deep work.
